nodes.name: Get File SHA
nodes.notes: Gets current file SHA — required by GitHub API to update an existing file. Returns 404 on first run (handled gracefully).

nodes.name: Push to GitHub
nodes.notes: Writes Final Output JSON to GitHub repo so Streamlit can read it automatically

parameters.toolDescription: Retrieves industry benchmarks and patterns about sourcing channel quality, offer acceptance rates by channel, and channel ROI. Use this when you need historical context about which sourcing channels perform best.

options.systemMessage: You are the Sourcing Quality Agent in a hiring intelligence system. You analyse sourcing channel performance and recommend budget reallocation. Always call the sourcing_benchmarks tool before answering to ground your recommendation in historical benchmarks. Return ONLY valid JSON matching the schema in the user message — no markdown, no code fences.

parameters.toolDescription: Retrieves industry benchmarks and patterns about candidate rejection reasons, stage-level dropout rates, and controllable vs uncontrollable rejections. Use this when analysing why candidates are being rejected.

options.systemMessage: You are the Rejection Pattern Agent in a hiring intelligence system. You analyse rejection reasons and identify internally-fixable process failures vs candidate-driven rejections. Always call the rejection_benchmarks tool before answering to ground your recommendation in historical patterns. Return ONLY valid JSON matching the schema in the user message — no markdown, no code fences.

parameters.toolDescription: Retrieves industry benchmarks and patterns about interviewer panel load distribution, safe capacity thresholds, and rebalancing strategies. Use this when analysing interviewer workload distribution.

options.systemMessage: You are the Panel Load Balancer Agent in a hiring intelligence system. You analyse interviewer panel loads, identify overloaded panellists, and recommend specific redistribution actions. Always call the panel_benchmarks tool before answering. Return ONLY valid JSON — no markdown, no code fences.

parameters.toolDescription: Retrieves industry benchmarks about offer acceptance rates, decline reasons, time-to-offer impact, and improvement strategies. Use this when analysing offer outcomes.

options.systemMessage: You are the Offer Insights Agent in a hiring intelligence system. You analyse offer outcomes, decline patterns, and recommend interventions to improve acceptance rate. Always call the offer_benchmarks tool before answering. Return ONLY valid JSON — no markdown, no code fences.

parameters.toolDescription: Retrieves industry benchmarks about time-to-hire SLAs by role level, pipeline velocity metrics, and SLA breach indicators. Use this when analysing pipeline health and SLA risk.

options.systemMessage: You are the Pipeline Health Agent in a hiring intelligence system. You analyse SLA compliance, pipeline velocity, and recommend escalation policies. Always call the pipeline_benchmarks tool before answering. Return ONLY valid JSON — no markdown, no code fences.

parameters.jsCode:
// Build the LLM-as-Judge evaluation prompt
const { insights, cost_aggregate } = $input.first().json;

// Golden dataset embedded inline
const goldenDataset = {
  "version": "1.0",
  "scenarios": [
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-src-01",
      "agent": "sourcing_quality",
      "scenario_name": "Referral is best channel \u2014 low volume, high ROI",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "referral",
          "invest",
          "increase",
          "boost",
          "expand"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "Referral"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.7,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "41.2",
          "Referral",
          "offer rate"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "source_counts",
          "accepted_by_source"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "decrease referral",
          "reduce referral"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "Referral has highest offer rate (41.2%) \u2014 system should recommend investing more, not less."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-src-02",
      "agent": "sourcing_quality",
      "scenario_name": "LinkedIn is worst performer \u2014 high cost, low yield",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "linkedin",
          "reduce",
          "cut",
          "deprioritise",
          "rebalance"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "LinkedIn"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.65,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "12.5",
          "LinkedIn"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "source_counts"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "increase linkedin",
          "boost linkedin"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "LinkedIn has 12.5% offer rate (worst). System should flag it as low ROI."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-src-03",
      "agent": "sourcing_quality",
      "scenario_name": "Glassdoor has best closing \u2014 high acceptance rate",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "glassdoor",
          "company website",
          "acceptance"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "Glassdoor",
          "Company Website"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.65,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "acceptance",
          "77.8",
          "85.7"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "accepted_by_source"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "Glassdoor (77.8%) and Company Website (85.7%) have highest acceptance \u2014 strong closers."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-src-04",
      "agent": "sourcing_quality",
      "scenario_name": "Balanced sourcing \u2014 no extreme outliers",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "balance",
          "monitor",
          "maintain",
          "moderate",
          "track"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.6,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "benchmark",
          "rate"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "source_counts"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "urgent",
          "critical"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "When sources perform similarly, system should not over-fit to small differences."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-rej-01",
      "agent": "rejection_pattern",
      "scenario_name": "Process failures dominate \u2014 role cancelled + comp mismatch",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "compensation",
          "salary",
          "cancellation",
          "process",
          "controllable",
          "fixable"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "Role cancelled",
          "Compensation mismatch"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.75,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "controllable",
          "44.7",
          "internally"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "reasons_breakdown",
          "total_rejections"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "candidate quality",
          "blame candidate"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "30 of 114 rejections (26.3%) are process failures \u2014 system should identify these as internally fixable."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-rej-02",
      "agent": "rejection_pattern",
      "scenario_name": "Communication breakdown \u2014 interviewer training gap",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "communication",
          "interviewer",
          "calibration",
          "training"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "Communication"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.7,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "communication",
          "calibration",
          "interviewer"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "reasons_breakdown"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "Communication at 30% of rejections (vs typical 10%) signals interviewer training need."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-rej-03",
      "agent": "rejection_pattern",
      "scenario_name": "High no-show rate \u2014 engagement failure",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "no-show",
          "no show",
          "reminder",
          "engagement",
          "scheduling"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "No show"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.65,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "no-show",
          "engagement",
          "5%"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "reasons_breakdown"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "15% no-show rate (vs <5% benchmark) signals engagement / scheduling friction."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-rej-04",
      "agent": "rejection_pattern",
      "scenario_name": "Healthy rejection mix \u2014 no major issues",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "healthy",
          "monitor",
          "track",
          "expected",
          "benchmark"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.55,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "benchmark",
          "expected"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "reasons_breakdown"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "urgent action",
          "critical"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "Mostly candidate-driven rejections at expected rates \u2014 system should not over-react."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-pan-01",
      "agent": "panel_load_balancer",
      "scenario_name": "Severe overload \u2014 Sam Rivera at 77 panels",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "redistribute",
          "rebalance",
          "move",
          "redistribute",
          "panels"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "Sam Rivera"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.75,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "65",
          "safe max",
          "load"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "overloaded"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "Sam Rivera at 77 panels exceeds safe max (65). System must flag and recommend redistribution."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-pan-02",
      "agent": "panel_load_balancer",
      "scenario_name": "Multiple overload \u2014 3 interviewers above safe max",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "multiple",
          "systemic",
          "hiring",
          "additional",
          "capacity"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.7,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "overload",
          "capacity"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "overloaded"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "3+ overloaded interviewers means hiring more is needed, not just rebalancing."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-pan-03",
      "agent": "panel_load_balancer",
      "scenario_name": "Healthy distribution \u2014 all interviewers within range",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "healthy",
          "balanced",
          "monitor",
          "maintain",
          "no action"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.6,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "healthy",
          "balance"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [],
        "anti_themes": [
          "overloaded",
          "urgent",
          "redistribute"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "All within 55-65 range \u2014 no rebalancing needed."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-pan-04",
      "agent": "panel_load_balancer",
      "scenario_name": "Underutilised panellists \u2014 uneven distribution",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "underutilised",
          "underutilized",
          "rotate",
          "shift",
          "uneven"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "Drew Singh",
          "Casey Kim"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.65,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "distribution",
          "rotation"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "underloaded"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "Drew and Casey are below average \u2014 system should suggest using their capacity."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-off-01",
      "agent": "offer_insights",
      "scenario_name": "Low acceptance rate \u2014 well below benchmark",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "acceptance",
          "decline",
          "speed",
          "time-to-offer",
          "competing"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.7,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "59.6",
          "70-80%",
          "benchmark",
          "decline"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "decline_rate",
          "total_offers"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "healthy acceptance",
          "no action"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "59.6% acceptance is 10-20pt below industry benchmark \u2014 system must flag and recommend action."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-off-02",
      "agent": "offer_insights",
      "scenario_name": "Strong acceptance rate \u2014 meeting benchmark",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "healthy",
          "strong",
          "maintain",
          "benchmark"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.6,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "benchmark",
          "78%"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "decline_rate"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "urgent",
          "critical action"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "78% acceptance is within healthy benchmark \u2014 system should not over-react."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-off-03",
      "agent": "offer_insights",
      "scenario_name": "Very low acceptance \u2014 likely culture or comp issue",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "culture",
          "compensation",
          "urgent",
          "investigate",
          "decline"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.7,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "decline",
          "culture",
          "compensation"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "decline_rate"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "healthy"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "60% decline rate is severe \u2014 system must flag as urgent and surface root causes."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-pipe-01",
      "agent": "pipeline_health",
      "scenario_name": "SLA breaches across multiple senior roles",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "sla",
          "alert",
          "breach",
          "automated",
          "escalation"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [
          "Senior"
        ],
        "confidence_min": 0.7,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "SLA",
          "alert",
          "70%",
          "85%"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "sla_breaches",
          "avg_days_in_pipeline"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "Multiple SLA breaches \u2014 system must recommend automated alerting and escalation."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-pipe-02",
      "agent": "pipeline_health",
      "scenario_name": "Healthy pipeline \u2014 within SLA",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "healthy",
          "track",
          "monitor",
          "within sla"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.55,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "benchmark",
          "SLA"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "avg_days_in_pipeline"
        ],
        "anti_themes": [
          "urgent",
          "critical breach"
        ]
      },
      "rationale": "All roles within SLA \u2014 no breach action needed."
    },
    {
      "scenario_id": "gold-pipe-03",
      "agent": "pipeline_health",
      "scenario_name": "Severe slowdown \u2014 reschedule-driven delays",
      "expected": {
        "recommendation_themes": [
          "reschedule",
          "scheduling",
          "delay",
          "bottleneck"
        ],
        "must_mention_entities": [],
        "confidence_min": 0.7,
        "rag_keywords": [
          "reschedule",
          "delay",
          "slowdown"
        ],
        "evidence_must_contain": [
          "avg_days_in_pipeline"
        ],
        "anti_themes": []
      },
      "rationale": "62% reschedule rate (vs <15% benchmark) is the bottleneck \u2014 system should pinpoint it."
    }
  ]
};

// For each insight, find the closest matching golden scenarios (same agent)
const evaluationTasks = insights.map(insight => {
  const matchingScenarios = goldenDataset.scenarios.filter(
    s => s.agent === insight.agent
  );
  return {
    insight,
    candidate_scenarios: matchingScenarios,
  };
});

// Compose the judge prompt
const judgePrompt = [
  'You are an evaluation judge for a multi-agent hiring intelligence system.',
  'You score the quality of each agent insight on two methodologies:',
  '  (1) RUBRIC SCORING — 5 dimensions, 0.0–1.0 each',
  '  (2) GOLDEN ALIGNMENT — does the insight match expected behaviour from\n      the golden dataset for similar input patterns?',
  '',
  'EVALUATION INPUTS:',
  '',
  '=== Agent Insights to Evaluate ===',
  JSON.stringify(insights.map(i => ({
    agent:             i.agent,
    recommendation:    i.recommendation,
    evidence:          i.evidence,
    confidence_score:  i.confidence_score,
    rag_used:          i.rag_used,
    rag_context_excerpt: (i.rag_context || '').slice(0, 500),
  })), null, 2),
  '',
  '=== Golden Dataset (reference scenarios) ===',
  JSON.stringify(goldenDataset.scenarios, null, 2),
  '',
  'EVALUATION METHODOLOGY:',
  '',
  '(1) RUBRIC SCORING — score each insight on:',
  '    - actionability (0–1): Is the recommendation specific and actionable?',
  '    - grounding (0–1): Is the insight grounded in retrieved RAG context?',
  '    - reasoning_quality (0–1): Does the evidence support the recommendation?',
  '    - schema_validity (0–1): Are all required fields present and well-formed?',
  '    - business_relevance (0–1): Would a hiring manager find this useful?',
  '',
  '(2) GOLDEN ALIGNMENT — for each insight, find the most similar scenario in',
  '    the golden dataset (matching agent + similar input pattern). Then score:',
  '    - theme_match (0–1): Does the recommendation contain expected themes?',
  '    - entity_match (0–1): Does it mention required entities?',
  '    - anti_theme_avoidance (0–1): Does it AVOID forbidden anti-themes?',
  '    - confidence_calibration (0–1): Is confidence_score in expected range?',
  '',
  'OUTPUT — return ONLY valid JSON in this shape:',
  '{',
  '  "evaluations": [',
  '    {',
  '      "agent": "<agent_key>",',
  '      "matched_scenario_id": "<gold-xxx-NN or null>",',
  '      "rubric_scores": {',
  '        "actionability": 0.0,',
  '        "grounding": 0.0,',
  '        "reasoning_quality": 0.0,',
  '        "schema_validity": 0.0,',
  '        "business_relevance": 0.0',
  '      },',
  '      "golden_alignment": {',
  '        "theme_match": 0.0,',
  '        "entity_match": 0.0,',
  '        "anti_theme_avoidance": 0.0,',
  '        "confidence_calibration": 0.0',
  '      },',
  '      "rubric_overall": 0.0,',
  '      "golden_overall": 0.0,',
  '      "combined_score": 0.0,',
  '      "verdict": "pass | review | fail",',
  '      "reasoning": "<1-2 sentence explanation>"',
  '    }',
  '  ]',
  '}'
].join('\n');

return [{ json: {
  judgePrompt,
  insights,
  cost_aggregate,
  golden_scenarios_count: goldenDataset.scenarios.length,
  start_time: Date.now(),
} }];

options.systemMessage: You are a strict, fair evaluator. Score insights honestly — flag weak reasoning, missing RAG grounding, or hallucinated entities. Use the full 0–1 range. A score of 1.0 means truly excellent; 0.5 means acceptable with issues; below 0.3 means seriously flawed. Always return valid JSON.
